Home

Blogroll

Here's a loosely categorised, irregularly updated collection of the feeds I follow. Because the internet was created to be a library, not a shopping centre. Descriptions are from the feeds themselves, so sorry if they're not very descriptive.

Comics

  • Cat and Girl

    Feed for Cat and Girl

  • David Revoy

    Artist, Instructor, using only Free/Libre and Open-Source software since 2009.

    Feed for David Revoy

  • ELECTRIC BONES ⚡

    a webcomic by hazel + bell

    Feed for ELECTRIC BONES ⚡

  • Existential Comics

    Existential Comics: A comic about the inevitable anguish of living a brief life in an absurd world. Also jokes.

    Feed for Existential Comics

  • EXTRA FABULOUS COMICS

    patreon.com/extrafabulouscomics

    Feed for EXTRA FABULOUS COMICS

  • Giant in the playground

    Order of the Stick

    Feed for Giant in the playground

  • HEARTSTOPPER

    HEARTSTOPPER is a webcomic by Alice Oseman. Updates on the 1st, 11th, and 21st of each month at 11am UK time. Also available to read on Tapas and Webtoon.

    Feed for HEARTSTOPPER

  • I'm Fine I'm Fine Just Understand

    Quiet comics about gender, mental health, and getting older.

    Feed for I'm Fine I'm Fine Just Understand

  • ILLUSTRATED by Alexander Naughton

    Graphic mumblings and ravings. Illustrated dissections of life. A merry chase.

    Feed for ILLUSTRATED by Alexander Naughton

  • Last Place Comics

    Feed for Last Place Comics

  • Oglaf!

    Comics. Often dirty. Updates Sundays.

    Feed for Oglaf!

  • Poorly Drawn Lines

    Comics by Reza Farazmand

    Feed for Poorly Drawn Lines

  • QC RSS

    The Official QC RSS Feed

    Feed for QC RSS

  • Sam And Fuzzy

    Sam And Fuzzy: New comics Monday, Wednesday, and Friday.

    Feed for Sam And Fuzzy

  • Saturday Morning Breakfast Cereal

    Latest Saturday Morning Breakfast Cereal comics and news

    Feed for Saturday Morning Breakfast Cereal

  • The Secret Knots

    Comics by Juan Santapau

    Feed for The Secret Knots

    the same joke every time by alex norris

    Feed for webcomic name

    Feed for xkcd

Entertaining

  • Heart Wired

    Weekly Attempts at Contemplation and Comedy

    Feed for Heart Wired

  • keen about travelling

    A 24 year old’s travels, escaping the corporate ladder (a break taken after a long three year career) and fundamentally a digital diary, enjoy x

    Feed for keen about travelling

  • Naive Weekly

    A Sunday email with links to the quiet, odd and poetic web.

    Feed for Naive Weekly

  • Orbis Tertius

    Eclectic esoterica, etcetera.

    Feed for Orbis Tertius

  • Shoddy Goods, from meh.com, by Jason Toon

    A newsletter from meh.com, by Jason Toon, about the stories behind the stuff people make, buy, and sell

    Feed for Shoddy Goods, from meh.com, by Jason Toon

  • Stat Significant

    Data-centric essays about movies, music, TV, and more.

    Feed for Stat Significant

  • The Lucyverse

    I review everything that comes out of the sewage pipe of capitalism!

    Feed for The Lucyverse

  • The Pudding

    The Pudding is an editorial publication that explains ideas debated in culture with visual essays.

    Feed for The Pudding

    Excellent writing about the physical world.

    Feed for Scope of Work

  • The Quantum Cat

  • University of Winds

    Three links to wonderful and thought-provoking things every other Saturday morning from Mita Williams.

    Feed for University of Winds

    the only substack that is about weird medieval guys :)

    Feed for weird medieval guys

  • Writing ＆ Such

    Feed for Writing ＆ Such

Links & Recs

  • 10+1 Things

    11 interesting stories handpicked by a curious human's algorithm.

    Feed for 10+1 Things

  • Five things on Friday

    <p>Established in 2011, <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://www.fivethingsonfriday.com/archive/">Five things on Friday</a> is a weekly/occasional newsletter written by me, <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/whatleydude/">James Whatley</a>.</p><p>It tries to be delivered to your inbox on Fridays but it often fails. Sometimes it arrives late. Sometimes it doesn’t arrive at all. But hey, it’s the thought that counts. </p><p>By day, I am an integrated strategist/planner working in advertising and communications. Often at the intersection of digital, entertainment, and brand.</p><p>Outside of working hours, I am a proud Dad, ardent gamer, and a regular roaster of potatoes.</p><p>As an interested human interested in interesting things, Five things on Friday is my way of a) collating the most interesting things I find on the internet and b) thinking out loud while reflecting on those things. </p><p>Along with work, life, culture and basically anything I feel like writing about.</p><p>If you're not sure then can find previous editions <a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" href="https://buttondown.email/fivethingsonfriday/archive/">right here</a> but lots of people tell me they like it so you might too.</p><p>So that’s my pitch.</p><p>Five things on Friday. It doesn’t always come on Friday but you always get more than five things.</p><p>Want in?</p>

    Feed for Five things on Friday

  • Five Trivia in a Minute

    A short collection of five trivia items on a random topic, that can be read in less than a minute.

    Feed for Five Trivia in a Minute

  • Links I Would Gchat You If We Were Friends

    A newsletter for curious, thoughtful readers who miss the “old” internet.

    Feed for Links I Would Gchat You If We Were Friends

    Atom feed for unread entries

    Feed for Wallabag

  • Brand Anatomy

    A Marketing Doctor's (MD) do's and don'ts of brand strategy.

    Feed for Tone Knob

Political

  • all the heterosexual nonsense

    A culture, news and comedy newsletter, with a focus on queer culture, news and issues from Rebecca Shaw and Patrick Lenton.

    Feed for all the heterosexual nonsense

    Funny feelings, weird careers, making art, American history, and how to be alive.

  • Jason Pargin's Newsletter

    Something seems off about the world, I'm trying to get to the bottom of it

    Feed for Jason Pargin's Newsletter

  • Number One With A Bullard

    Notes on our nostalgic era. Pop culture anxieties. Pieces of history. Occasional jokes.

    Feed for Number One With A Bullard

  • Pluralistic: Daily links from Cory Doctorow

    No trackers, no ads. Black type, white background. Privacy policy: we don't collect or retain any data at all ever period.

    Feed for Pluralistic: Daily links from Cory Doctorow

  • Read Max

    Explaining the weird new future, one newsletter at a time. Subscribe for a twice weekly delivery of internet culture, mega-platform grotesquerie, crypto conspiracies, deep forum lore, fringe politics, and other artifacts of what's to come.

    Feed for Read Max

  • Sheffield Tribune

    Sheffield's quality newspaper, delivered via email.

    Feed for Sheffield Tribune

  • The Culture Shock

    Obsessively interested in everything.

    Feed for The Culture Shock

  • The Index

    Notes on tech + culture + politics.

    Feed for The Index

    A newsletter about taking control of our attention and finding a more human side of the internet. Featured in Wired and Rolling Stone.

    Feed for escape the algorithm

  • Escaping Flatland

    I write essays about relationships, thought, and agency. I hang with our kids. We live on a pine covered island in the Baltic Sea. That's about it.

    • CSS Tip: Learn CSS the easy way

      The best place to keep up to date with the new CSS features. Daily CSS tips and tricks to become a better web developer.

    • Go Make Things

      Chris Ferdinandi is a web developer with ADHD, helping people build the web better.

      Feed for Go Make Things

    • Harry Cresswell Links

      Recent content in Links on Harry Cresswell

      Feed for Harry Cresswell Links

    • Harry Cresswell Newsletter

      Recent content in Newsletter on Harry Cresswell

      Feed for Harry Cresswell Newsletter

    • Ludicity

      <blockquote> "While I'm deeply sympathetic, the author should be discussing their issues with a therapist rather than spreading this on the internet." </blockquote> I finally rage-quit and started an IT consultancy, Hermit Tech. If you're an engineer whose company is doing crazy things and there might still be room for an intervention (i.e, there must be at least one sane person in leadership), you can book time directly on my calendar and we can figure out a game plan! I've worked with some readers before and everyone had a good time. We've developed bold, innovative technology, such as not lying, the ability to explain things to laymen, and actually knowing how to ship on time. If you live in Melbourne, this final link straight-up arranges an in-person meeting at a nice coffee place. I also talk to most readers who want to chat, but you'll have to email me. I read all mail without exception, and love getting it. I may not respond, but I'll always read! You can reach me at: ludicity.hackernews@gmail.com. My podcast, "Does A Frog Have Scorpion Nature", where I talk to people I like, and sometimes find blog post inspiration. You can also catch me on Mastodon. Liberapay | Patreon to support me writing taking time off to write amidst the difficulty of launching a business.

      Feed for Ludicity

    • The Index by Piccalilli

      High quality, curated design, dev and tech links. Twice a week.

      Feed for The Index by Piccalilli

    • Web Weekly

      A weekly newsletter about web development, tooling, and the latest trends in the web platform.

      Feed for Web Weekly

    • Working Notes

      A short and useful newsletter for people who look after websites.

      Feed for Working Notes
